spec.name: Barrow Hall
spec.description: The passage opens into a low stone chamber, roughly circular, its ceiling formed by several large capstones. The space is tight — five or six paces across — lit only by whatever light comes in from the entrance. Stone shelves line the walls, once holding grave goods of which only fragmentary traces remain: a corroded fitting, a scatter of pot shards. The centre of the floor is packed hard, and faint carvings cover the wall stones — geometric patterns that repeat in an ordered way that suggests meaning rather than decoration.
